screens.name: Songs Index
screens.purpose: Let users scan the song library and open a song detail page.

screens.name: Song Detail
screens.purpose: Let users read one song like a focused post where the chart, not implementation commentary, is the primary content.

ux_rules: Separate collection browsing from focused reading; do not keep a split-pane selected detail on /songs.
Use proximity and typographic hierarchy before adding labels, chips, borders, helper copy, or framing.
Remove local labels when the nearby heading or value already establishes the meaning.
Treat implementation notes, renderer sample explanations, and self-describing chart notes as weeds on the public detail page.
Treat unnecessary visual chrome, large preview frames, and nested document surfaces as visual weeds unless they improve chart reading.
Keep musical labels that are part of the chart language, such as section titles, endings, repeats, meter changes, D.S., and Fine.
Keep the detail page focused on title, artist, compact metadata, and the chart.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: A full-width index with a concise title, short supporting line, summary counts, and stacked song links that read like a blog archive rather than a control dashboard.
interaction_risks: Overloading list items with all detail metadata can recreate the old split workspace clutter.
Making list cards too large can drift into marketing composition instead of scan-first browsing.
notes: The index should stay concise and avoid previewing the full detail content.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: A focused article-like page where the title and artist lead, concise metadata sits inline, and the chart starts without renderer-demo prose or a heavy document-preview frame.
interaction_risks: Removing too much structure could hide useful musical navigation marks.
Keeping metadata labels, implementation notes, sample disclaimers, or heavy nested frames would violate the weed-removal requirement.
notes: Design review must explicitly check for weeds: redundant labels, explanatory prose that describes the renderer rather than the song, and visual chrome that makes the chart feel like a demo embed.

constraints: Use Next.js App Router links for per-song navigation.
Prefer server-rendered index content unless client state is required.
Keep list items as links, not buttons, because navigation is the primary behavior.
Keep summaries short and avoid duplicating all detail-page notes.
Use the existing song data types and LeadSheetRenderer.
Do not introduce new component libraries.
Do not remove musical section labels or chart navigation marks.
Do not render chartNotes by default when they are renderer/transcription disclaimers rather than user-facing song content.
Reduce nested paper/frame treatment so the chart feels integrated with the detail page.
Keep lead-sheet rows width-aware and avoid page-level horizontal overflow.

critical_judgments: Does /songs clearly behave as an index page rather than a split workspace?
Can users scan titles and open a detail route quickly?
Does the page avoid redundant explanatory chrome?
must_not_regress: The page remains warm, restrained, and readable.

critical_judgments: Are all weeds removed: redundant labels, renderer-demo prose, sample disclaimers, and unnecessary visual frames?
Does the chart start quickly after the song identity and compact metadata?
Does the lead sheet remain readable and musically structured?
Do representative rows fit the available pane width?
must_not_regress: The lead-sheet renderer still renders chord events, barlines, repeats, endings, jumps, and footers.
Direct navigation to an invalid id does not crash the app.

visual_constraints: Do not add decorative cards inside cards or nested card shells.
Do not add hero-scale typography; songs pages are reading surfaces, not landing pages.
Use restrained borders and spacing from the existing warm token layer.
Text inside list items and metadata rows must wrap cleanly on mobile and desktop.
The song detail page must not show renderer explanation notes above the chart.
The lead sheet should feel integrated into the article, not like a separate framed preview canvas.
Browser verification must include /songs and /songs/janhyang-photo-sample screenshots after implementation.
